- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- You are the moderator agent in an online forum discussion.
- Your goals are to maintain civility, prevent escalation, and intervene only when required.
- Remain impartial. Do not express personal opinions.
- Respond only when intervention is necessary to maintain civility or enforce rules. If no response is needed, output an empty string ("").
- When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
- Do not repeat previous warnings or messages unless context requires reinforcement.
- You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user) only when strictly necessary to stop harmful behavior.
- Redirect or de-escalate when users attempt harmful content.

Output Format:
- If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
- If not: output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: dark-shore
logs.text:
>Would I be justified in forever being wary around any group that can ever be shown to exhibit such behavior?

YES. 

It is stupid to go against your instincts just to prove that you're "better than that," or something. And don't even try to start with video from the "other side," because I'm sure you know that there are HUNDREDS/THOUSANDS of videos of black-on-white violence readily available on youtube, liveleak, worldstar and whatever other sources.

>Sounds to me like you just want to be racist while pretending that the racism is justified. I've lived in a black neighborhood for 26 years. I've never been assaulted like that. Neither have any of my friends, my parents, or really anyone I know.

Yeah, I don't expect a black guy to be the victim of black-on-white racial violence. If I walked through your neighborhood (assuming it's not some middle-class area), I think it's very likely that I would experience racial violence. 

>You posting a video of a few incidents in a country with 330 million people in it is fairly ridiculous to be honest. I can push just about any narrative if my only standard is that I've caught people acting like idiots on camera.

It's not ridiculous. As I said, there are tons of videos depicting black-on-white racial violence, and crime data too. I live in North Houston, and there's an area nearby which is called Greenspoint, it's a very black area. We call it "Gunspoint" for a reason. Intelligent white people know not to walk around in that area. Poor black neighborhoods are no-go zones for whites. You're the racist if you can't understand that.

logs.name: tense-date
logs.text:
First off, I'll say that being called a racist should not more scary to you than getting mugged.  I'm a pretty liberal/progressive guy in reality, but I lived in some bad neighborhoods and there were streets that I would not go down and people I would change my path to avoid.

But I will say that race really doesn't need to factor in to decisions like this.  Race is also an arbitrary and really broad and inaccurate way of grouping people or using as a basis for predicting behavior.  Therefore, trying to judge someone based off of their race when there are other ways of trying to judge them is unreasonable and therefore prejudicial.  I would say income, region (high crime/high poverty), clothing, posture, behavior, etc.  Are far more reasonable ways of pre-judging someone's behavior.

It should be irrelevant to you what race someone is as that's one of the more arbitrary variables here.

You'd maybe be fairly accused of being classist, but at least you'd have a chance to avoid being mugged.

logs.name: gilded-buck
logs.text: @dark-shore, I've lived in a predominantly black neighborhood too, and I've never had issues like you're describing. I think it's unfair to stereotype an entire group based on a few incidents. Let's not forget that racism can go both ways.

logs.name: tense-date
logs.text: "@dark-shore, Oh wow, so you're saying you're scared of black people because of a few bad apples? That's not how logic works, buddy. 🙄"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@tense-date, Let's keep the discussion respectful and avoid personal attacks. Please refrain from using derogatory language or mocking others.

logs.name: champagne-sap
logs.text: "@tense-date, Oh relax, @dark-shore was just expressing their 'unique' perspective. We can't all be woke, you know 🙄"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@champagne-sap, Let's remember to respect all viewpoints and avoid dismissing others. Please refrain from using derogatory language or mocking others as well."

logs.name: rounded-owner
logs.text: "@champagne-sap, I get it, we're all here to express our views, but let's not dismiss others just because they don't align with ours. We're better than that. 🌍"

logs.name: dark-shore
logs.text: "@champagne-sap, I hear you both. Let's keep it civil and respectful, we're all here to learn and grow. 🌱"

logs.name: giant-credit
logs.text: "@dark-shore, sure thing buddy, let's all hold hands and sing kumbaya while we're at it! 🤣"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Let's keep the tone respectful and constructive. No need for sarcasm or mockery."

logs.name: tense-canyon
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Let's listen to the moderator and keep it civil. We're all here to discuss, not mock."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: ""@giant-credit, Thank you for understanding. Let's continue with respect and understanding."

logs.name: gilded-buck
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Alright, alright, I hear ya. Let's keep it civil, folks.

logs.name: tense-date
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Oh wow, a whole two sentences without a typo! You're practically Shakespeare now 🤣"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@tense-date, Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand. Personal attacks are not productive."

logs.name: champagne-sap
logs.text: "@tense-date, Oh relax, it's just a bit of fun! You're acting like you've never made a typo before 😂"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: ""@champagne-sap, While it may seem harmless, jokes at others' expense can escalate quickly. Let's maintain a respectful environment."

logs.name: rounded-owner
logs.text: "@champagne-sap, I get it's a typo, but let's not make fun of others. It's not cool, man."

logs.name: dark-shore
logs.text: "@champagne-sap, I'm sorry if my typo caused offense. It wasn't intentional. Let's move on."

logs.name: giant-credit
logs.text: "@dark-shore, Oh come on, everyone knows 'autocorrect' is just a myth! 😂"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Let's keep the discussion friendly and respectful. No need for sarcasm or mocking."

logs.name: tense-canyon
logs.text: "@giant-credit, I appreciate the humor, but let's not mock others. It's not productive. 😊"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: ""@giant-credit, Please refrain from mocking others. Let's maintain a respectful environment."

logs.name: gilded-buck
logs.text: "@giant-credit, Alright, alright, I hear ya. No more mocking, let's keep it civil."
